title: Motor Job Record v1.1
description: One execution of a recipe, and simultaneously its receipt. The core design rule: separate truth fields. 'Process alive' != 'job completed'; 'dispatch happened' != 'execution succeeded'; 'receipt stale' != 'execution failed'; 'tests passed' != 'production deployed'; 'PR created' != 'business outcome achieved'; 'outcome observed' != 'outcome attributable to this candidate'. Each of those is its own field with its own evidence. v1.1 makes the previously-prose invariants machine-checkable: PROVEN/FAILED require evidence_ref; BLOCKED/FAILED/DIVERGED/UNPROVEN require reason; promotion splits into source+runtime; outcome splits into observation+attribution.

job_id.description: E.g. MOTOR-WEB-001. Assigned at creation, immutable.

idempotency_key.description: Rendered from recipe trigger.dedupe_key. Same key = same job, never a duplicate (L13). Required + non-empty so U dedup can never be silently skipped.

authorized_by.description: Authority that admitted the job (may differ from requester).

instruction_ref.description: Link/ID of the instruction, issue, or event payload.

states.description: THE separated truth fields. Every field carries its own evidence pointer; no field may be inferred from another.

dispatch.description: Was the job handed to a worker? (RUNNING_CONFIRMED-adjacent; says nothing about completion.)

execution.description: Did the worker actually run and finish? Distinct from dispatch — the NOOS incident lived exactly in this gap (DISPATCHING_COMPLETION_UNPROVEN).

verification.description: Did declared checks pass? PASS valid only with evidence; external checks only from probes the builder doesn't control (L4).

evidence.description: Were receipts written AND readable by the observer? Stale here means INSUFFICIENT_RECENT_EVIDENCE, success_rate=null — never inferred failure.

authority.description: Has required human/policy approval been given? founder_blocked is a live state, never 'cancelled' (L7).

promotion.description: Was the accepted artifact promoted? SPLIT: source (merge to main) and runtime (production deploy) are different facts — 'merged' != 'deployed'. Tests passing does not touch this field.

outcome.description: Was the real-world result observed post-promotion? SPLIT: observation (the page shows new content) and attribution (that content was produced by THIS deployed SHA) are different facts. Only proven attribution closes the loop (≥60s after deploy).

recovery.description: Optional. If repair ran: what, and did an external check confirm it caused recovery? Correlation is not causality — self-healing is PROVEN only by a before/after external check pair.

lifecycle_status.description: Convenience roll-up for dashboards. Derived from states.*, never authored directly — the states object is the truth. validate.py enforces that RECEIPT_COMPLETE cannot be asserted while underlying states are incomplete.

log_ref.description: Pointer to captured stdout/stderr or transcript.

production_sha.description: Empty until states.promotion.runtime is PROVEN. When set, must be a full 40-hex SHA (validate.py I9).

evidence.description: Command + output, or probe URL + response markers + hash.

approval_artifact.description: The durable evidence: merge SHA, /approve comment URL. Verbal-only = not approval.

total_usd.description: Metered at the call site, not estimated after (L11). A placeholder zero MUST set metered=false; a RECEIPT_COMPLETE job MUST have metered=true (validate.py).

metered.description: True = total_usd is a real metered value. False/absent = placeholder; blocks RECEIPT_COMPLETE.

outcome_observed_at.description: Must be >=60s after promoted_at for external-proof recipes (validate.py I8 auto-reject otherwise).

receipt_hash.description: Hash of the canonical serialization of this record at RECEIPT_COMPLETE, for tamper-evidence. Required for RECEIPT_COMPLETE.

stateEnum.description: UNPROVEN = we believe it happened but lack evidence (maps to DISPATCHING_COMPLETION_UNPROVEN). STALE = evidence exists but is older than the freshness window. DIVERGED = observer and source disagree. These are three different facts; never collapse them into FAILED.

evidence_ref.description: Pointer to the receipt/log/probe output backing this state.

source.description: Merge/publish to the authoritative source (e.g. merge to main). Proven by a merge SHA.

runtime.description: Deploy to the running production target. Proven by a deployment receipt (deployment id + deployed SHA). 'merged' does NOT prove this.

observation.description: The external result was observed (page shows new content, endpoint returns marker).

attribution.description: That observed result is attributable to THIS candidate (deployed SHA == candidate SHA). Observation without attribution never closes the loop.
